generatedSentence: Nighthawk, an outlander from the highland, known for his outgoing nature despite an obvious secret, was an outlaw in a world where everyone was forced to obey a rigid system; his odd behavior and slight obesity were often subjects of mockery, yet his outlook remained strangely angelic, possessing a briefcase filled with orthoclase crystals, an ornament from his hereditary past, that powered a strange inverter, ensuring his efficiency to innovate solutions during the occasions when the international production schedule was broken, truly a visionary in this orderly but oppressive world. On a nocturnal mission, Nighthawk had to overcome his prior indifference when he began to overhear jargon about a dangerous chemical plot against the dynasty, receiving an instruction from an unknown source to reach a vessel before krakens could be unleashed, a mayday signal for his faithful team of rangers, his jumpsuit felt velvety but offered little protection, a subtle irony as he realized the jacquerie was afoot, the keynote of their election was a projected pestilence, designed to deject the populace, while the estimation of the turnout for their planned demolition was bloated, an obnoxious display of power. With his watchful eye, Nighthawk observed the jester dancing amidst various objects, a deliberate distraction while the officiant prepared a strange baptism for the offspring of the outlaw leader; these objects pulsed with neodymium energy, and the whiffed whiskey from a nearby playhouse added to the chaotic scene, where racoons were jumping around with an almost mechanical reflex, making the scene even more unusual and hinting at a deeper plot to carjack the very memory of the kingdom, forcing Nighthawk to act brief, using jujitsu to grab the glowing piece of jasper – the true power source – before it was legally used to legitimize the adjective of their usurped rule. He truly had to ensure he didn't overdo his moves, as he could barely float after the last breaker detonated, but with pure objectivity and the insights from his partner Johnny, he had kissed the notion of defeat goodbye, saving the day for the flock who could finally enjoy lemonade in their fancy new tourism district, leaving the underwear of the previous regime in the dust; it was an odd occasion, yet a testament to Nighthawk's reliability and the power of a single individual to avert shipwreck, even if he did oversleep through the victory parade, a small price for freedom.
panelSentences: Nighthawk, an outlander from the highland, known for his outgoing nature despite an obvious secret, was an outlaw in a world where everyone was forced to obey a rigid system; his odd behavior and slight obesity were often subjects of mockery, yet his outlook remained strangely angelic, possessing a briefcase filled with orthoclase crystals, an ornament from his hereditary past, that powered a strange inverter, ensuring his efficiency to innovate solutions during the occasions when the international production schedule was broken, truly a visionary in this orderly but oppressive world.

On a nocturnal mission, Nighthawk had to overcome his prior indifference when he began to overhear jargon about a dangerous chemical plot against the dynasty, receiving an instruction from an unknown source to reach a vessel before krakens could be unleashed, a mayday signal for his faithful team of rangers, his jumpsuit felt velvety but offered little protection, a subtle irony as he realized the jacquerie was afoot, the keynote of their election was a projected pestilence, designed to deject the populace, while the estimation of the turnout for their planned demolition was bloated, an obnoxious display of power.

With his watchful eye, Nighthawk observed the jester dancing amidst various objects, a deliberate distraction while the officiant prepared a strange baptism for the offspring of the outlaw leader; these objects pulsed with neodymium energy, and the whiffed whiskey from a nearby playhouse added to the chaotic scene, where racoons were jumping around with an almost mechanical reflex, making the scene even more unusual and hinting at a deeper plot to carjack the very memory of the kingdom, forcing Nighthawk to act brief, using jujitsu to grab the glowing piece of jasper – the true power source – before it was legally used to legitimize the adjective of their usurped rule.

He truly had to ensure he didn't overdo his moves, as he could barely float after the last breaker detonated, but with pure objectivity and the insights from his partner Johnny, he had kissed the notion of defeat goodbye, saving the day for the flock who could finally enjoy lemonade in their fancy new tourism district, leaving the underwear of the previous regime in the dust; it was an odd occasion, yet a testament to Nighthawk's reliability and the power of a single individual to avert shipwreck, even if he did oversleep through the victory parade, a small price for freedom.
rawPanelSentences: Nighthawk, an **outlander** from the **highland**, known for his **outgoing** nature despite an **obvious** secret, was an **outlaw** in a world where everyone was forced to **obey** a rigid **system**; his **odd** behavior and slight **obesity** were often subjects of **mockery**, yet his **outlook** remained strangely **angelic**, possessing a **briefcase** filled with **orthoclase** crystals, an **ornament** from his **hereditary** past, that powered a strange **inverter**, ensuring his **efficiency** to **innovate** solutions during the **occasions** when the **international** **production schedule** was **broken**, truly a **visionary** in this **orderly** but oppressive world.

On a **nocturnal** mission, Nighthawk had to **overcome** his prior **indifference** when he began to **overhear** **jargon** about a **dangerous** **chemical** plot against the **dynasty**, receiving an **instruction** from an unknown source to **reach** a **vessel** before **krakens** could be unleashed, a **mayday** signal for his **faithful** team of **rangers**, his **jumpsuit** felt **velvety** but offered little protection, a subtle **irony** as he realized the **jacquerie** was afoot, the **keynote** of their **election** was a **projected** **pestilence**, designed to **deject** the populace, while the **estimation** of the **turnout** for their planned **demolition** was **bloated**, an **obnoxious** display of power.

With his **watchful** eye, Nighthawk observed the **jester** **dancing** amidst various **objects**, a deliberate distraction while the **officiant** prepared a strange **baptism** for the **offspring** of the **outlaw** leader; these **objects** pulsed with **neodymium** energy, and the **whiffed** **whiskey** from a nearby **playhouse** added to the chaotic scene, where **racoons** were **jumping** around with an almost mechanical **reflex**, making the scene even more **unusual** and hinting at a deeper plot to **carjack** the very **memory** of the kingdom, forcing Nighthawk to act **brief**, using **jujitsu** to grab the glowing **piece** of **jasper** – the true power source – before it was **legally** used to legitimize the **adjective** of their usurped rule.

He truly had to ensure he didn't **overdo** his moves, as he could barely **float** after the last **breaker** detonated, but with pure **objectivity** and the insights from his partner **Johnny**, he had **kissed** the notion of defeat goodbye, saving the day for the **flock** who could finally enjoy **lemonade** in their **fancy** new **tourism** district, leaving the **underwear** of the previous regime in the dust; it was an odd **occasion**, yet a testament to Nighthawk's **reliability** and the power of a single individual to avert **shipwreck**, even if he did **oversleep** through the victory parade, a small price for freedom.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone figure, Nighthawk, wearing a dark armored jumpsuit with glowing blue accents and a silver visor helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk, a lone figure, examining a glowing orthoclase crystal in a high-tech briefcase, surrounded by the holographic projections of a city schedule, viewed from a low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Nighthawk, in a dynamic mid-air pose, leaping across a series of futuristic rooftops, looking down at a chaotic street scene with flashing police lights and shadowy figures, viewed from an eye-level medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk in a close-up, intensely focused, utilizing a precise jujitsu move to disarm a jester-like antagonist who is attempting to activate a glowing jasper artifact, viewed from a slightly high-angle close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Nighthawk, silhouetted against a vibrant, restored city skyline at dawn, holding a glass of lemonade, with a small, stylized raccoon character peeking from behind him, viewed from a wide-angle, slightly elevated perspective.

definitions.definition: A fugitive from the law.
(history) A criminal who is excluded from normal legal rights; one who can be killed at will without legal penalty.
A person who operates outside established norms.
definitions.example: The main character in the play was a bit of an outlaw who refused to shake hands or say thank you.

definitions.definition: A wild horse.
An in-law: a relative by marriage.
One who would be an in-law except that the marriage-like relationship is unofficial.
A prostitute who works alone, without a pimp.
To declare illegal.
To place a ban upon.
To remove from legal jurisdiction or enforcement.
definitions.example: to outlaw a debt or claim

definitions.definition: To deprive of legal force.
definitions.example: Laws outlawed by necessity. — Fuller.

definitions.definition: Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.
To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
To do as one is told.
To be obedient, compliant (to a given law, restriction etc.).
To go out, to set forth.
To go further; to exceed or surpass; go beyond.
To overtake; to travel faster than.
To outdo; exceed; surpass.
To come to an end.
The act of leaving or going out; exit, departure.
(chiefly in the plural) Money that leaves one's possession; expenditure, outlay, expense.
The extreme limit; the place of ending.
Extraverted: talkative, friendly, and social, especially with respect to meeting new people easily and comfortably.
definitions.example: Tom is very outgoing and enjoys meeting people; his brother, on the other hand, is painfully shy.

definitions.definition: Going out, on its way out.
definitions.example: Is there any outgoing post?

definitions.definition: Being replaced in office (while still in office but after election has determined that he/she will be replaced).
definitions.example: The outgoing politician wasn't too disappointed he'd lost the election; he was tired of political infighting.

definitions.definition: The burden or recurring theme in a song.
A surplus.
To surmount (a physical or abstract obstacle); to prevail over, to get the better of.
definitions.example: to overcome enemies in battle

definitions.definition: To win or prevail in some sort of battle, contest, etc.
definitions.example: We shall overcome.

definitions.definition: To come or pass over; to spread over.
definitions.example: I was overcome with anger.

definitions.definition: To overflow; to surcharge.
(diminutive) An odd number.
definitions.example: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.

definitions.definition: Something left over, not forming part of a set.
Differing from what is usual, ordinary or expected.
definitions.example: She slept in, which was very odd.

definitions.definition: Without a corresponding mate in a pair or set; unmatched; (of a pair or set) mismatched.
definitions.example: My cat Fluffy has odd eyes: one blue and one brown.

definitions.definition: Left over, remaining after the rest have been paired or grouped.
definitions.example: I'm the odd one out.

definitions.definition: Left over or remaining (as a small amount) after counting, payment, etc.
Scattered; occasional, infrequent; not forming part of a set or pattern.
definitions.example: I don't speak Latin well, so in hearing a dissertation in Latin, I would only be able to make out the odd word of it.

definitions.definition: Not regular or planned.
definitions.example: He's only worked odd jobs.

definitions.definition: Used or employed for odd jobs.
Numerically indivisible by two.
definitions.example: The product of odd numbers is also odd.

definitions.definition: Numbered with an odd number.
definitions.example: How do I print only the odd pages?

definitions.definition: (in combination with a number) About, approximately; somewhat more than (an approximated round number).
definitions.example: There were thirty-odd people in the room.

definitions.definition: Out of the way, secluded.
On the left.
definitions.example: He served from the odd court.

definitions.definition: Singular in excellence; matchless; peerless; outstanding.
Extremely unpleasant or offensive; very annoying, odious or contemptible.
definitions.example: He was an especially obnoxious and detestable specimen of a man.

definitions.definition: Exposing to harm or injury.
The state of being obese due to an excess of body fat.
A foreigner or alien.
A stranger or outsider.
A favorable opportunity; a convenient or timely chance.
definitions.example: At this point, she seized the occasion to make her own observation.

definitions.definition: The time when something happens.
definitions.example: on this occasion, I'm going to decline your offer, but next time I might agree.

definitions.definition: An occurrence or state of affairs which causes some event or reaction; a motive or reason.
definitions.example: I had no occasion to feel offended, however.

definitions.definition: Something which causes something else; a cause.
An occurrence or incident.
A particular happening; an instance or time when something occurred.
definitions.example: I could think of two separate occasions when she had deliberately lied to me.

definitions.definition: Need; requirement, necessity.
definitions.example: I have no occasion for firearms.

definitions.definition: A special event or function.
definitions.example: Having people round for dinner was always quite an occasion at our house.

definitions.definition: A reason or excuse; a motive; a persuasion.
To cause; to produce; to induce
definitions.example: it is seen that the mental changes are occasioned by a change of polarity

definitions.definition: The state of being objective, just, unbiased and not influenced by emotions or personal prejudices.
The world as it really is; reality.
A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.
(grammar) The noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase. In a verb phrase with a transitive action verb, it is typically the receiver of the action.
A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.
definitions.example: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

definitions.definition: An instantiation of a class or structure.
An element within a category upon which functions operate. Thus, a category consists of a set of element objects and the functions that operate on them.
Sight; show; appearance; aspect.
To hear something that was not meant for one's ears.
definitions.example: I was hanging clothes in the garden and I overheard the neighbours talking about Sheila's pregnancy.

definitions.definition: A sudden, often violent expression of emotion or activity.
To burst out.

Potassium aluminum silicate, KAlSi3O8, a common feldspar of igneous, plutonic, and metamorphic rocks. Orthoclase is the main feldspar of pegmatite occurrences, where it is most commonly flesh-colored. Orthoclase is used in the ceramic and glass industries and as a decorative gravel.
A hospital attendant given a variety of non-medical duties.
A soldier who carries out minor tasks for a superior officer.
Neat and tidy; possessing order.
definitions.example: He has always kept an orderly kitchen, with nothing out of place.

definitions.definition: Methodical or systematic.
definitions.example: We live in an orderly universe, where rules govern both the movements of planets and the binding of molecules.

definitions.definition: Peaceful; well-behaved.
definitions.example: An orderly gathering of citizens stood on the corner awaiting the bus.

definitions.definition: Being on duty; keeping order; conveying orders.
According to good order or practice; appropriately, in a well-behaved or orderly way.
In order; in a particular order or succession; with a suitable arrangement.
An element of decoration; that which embellishes or adorns.
A Christmas tree decoration.
A musical flourish that is unnecessary to the overall melodic or harmonic line, but serves to decorate or "ornament" that line.
(in the plural) The articles used in church services.
A characteristic that has a decorative function (typically in order to attract a mate)
To decorate.
definitions.example: We will ornament the windows with trim to make the room seem brighter.

definitions.definition: To add to.
definitions.example: The editor ornamented his plain writing, making it fancier but less clear.

definitions.definition: To sleep for longer than intended.
definitions.example: I overslept and was late for school.

definitions.definition: To sleep for longer than one intended.
To sleep beyond (a given time), to sleep through (an event etc.).
definitions.example: to oversleep one's usual hour of rising

definitions.definition: To do too much; to exceed what is proper or true in doing; to carry too far.
definitions.example: I overdid the sweets during the holidays and put on some weight.

definitions.definition: To cook for too long.
definitions.example: to overdo the meat

definitions.definition: To give (someone or something) too much work; to require too much effort or strength of (someone); to use up too much of (something).
definitions.example: to overdo one’s strength

definitions.definition: To do more than (someone); to do (something) to a greater extent.
A place from which something can be viewed.
definitions.example: Perched on the edge of the cliff was a hidden outlook.

definitions.definition: The view from such a place.
An attitude or point of view.
definitions.example: He has a positive outlook on life.

definitions.definition: Expectation for the future.
definitions.example: The outlook for temperature rises is worrying.

definitions.definition: To face or look in an outward direction.
To look at (someone) so long or intently that they look away; to win or prevail over (someone or something).
To be more attractive than (someone or something).
To inspect throughly; to select.
To look beyond (something).
A person who officiates at a religious ceremony (other than the Eucharist)
A person who officiates at a civil (non-religious) wedding ceremony.
The extent to which time is well used for the intended task.
definitions.example: The efficiency of the planning department is deplorable.

definitions.definition: The quality of producing an effect or effects.
The extent to which a resource, such as electricity, is used for the intended purpose; the ratio of useful work to energy expended.
definitions.example: The efficiency of this loudspeaker is 40%.

definitions.definition: A one-room apartment.
definitions.example: I have an efficiency available June through July.

definitions.definition: The act of instructing, teaching, or furnishing with information or knowledge.
definitions.example: Instruction will be provided on how to handle difficult customers.

definitions.definition: An instance of the information or knowledge so furnished.
An order or command.
A single operation of a processor defined by an instruction set architecture.
A set of directions provided by a manufacturer for the users of a product or service.
To alter, to change into something new; to revolutionize.
To introduce something new to a particular environment; to do something new.
To introduce (something) as new.
definitions.example: to innovate a word or an act

definitions.definition: Something that inverts, or causes inversion
A power inverter
Someone who has represented their country in a particular sport.
definitions.example: The United team includes five England internationals.

definitions.definition: A game or contest between two or more nations.
A transnational organization of political parties of similar ideology.
Of or having to do with more than one nation.
Between or among nations
definitions.example: an international discussion

definitions.definition: Participated in by two or more nations
definitions.example: an international competition

definitions.definition: Common to, or affecting, two or more nations.
definitions.example: an international rule

definitions.definition: Serving two or more nations
definitions.example: an international airport

definitions.definition: Of or concerning the association called the International.
Independent of national boundaries; common to all people.
definitions.example: The atmosphere is an international resource.

definitions.definition: Foreign; of another nation.
definitions.example: an international student

definitions.definition: Someone who has visions; a seer
An impractical dreamer
Someone who has positive ideas about the future
Having vision or foresight
Imaginary or illusory
Prophetic or revelatory
Idealistic or utopian
definitions.example: a visionary scheme or project

definitions.definition: As permitted by law; not contrary to law.
definitions.example: You can legally park in the lot on weekends without paying the fee, they won't ticket you.

definitions.definition: From a legal perspective.
definitions.example: Legally, I think you are covered, but there are angry guys with baseball bats outside.

definitions.definition: A flavoured beverage consisting of water, lemon, and sweetener, sometimes ice, served mainly as a refreshment.
A clear, usually carbonated, beverage made from lemon or artificial lemon flavouring, water, and sugar.
Recreational drugs of poor or weak quality, especially heroin.
The act of producing, making or creating something.
definitions.example: The widget making machine is being used for production now.

definitions.definition: The act of bringing something forward, out etc. for use or consideration.
The act of being produced.
definitions.example: The widgets are coming out of production now.

definitions.definition: The total amount produced.
definitions.example: They hope to increase spaghetti production next year.

definitions.definition: The presentation of a theatrical work.
definitions.example: We went to a production of Hamlet.

definitions.definition: An occasion or activity made more complicated than necessary.
definitions.example: He made a simple meal into a huge production.

definitions.definition: That which is manufactured or is ready for manufacturing in volume (as opposed to a prototype or conceptual model).
definitions.example: This is the final production model.

definitions.definition: The act of lengthening out or prolonging.
An extension or protrusion.
A rewrite rule specifying a symbol substitution that can be recursively performed to generate new symbol sequences. (More information on Wikipedia.)
definitions.example: Each production is implemented with a function.

definitions.definition: The environment where finished code runs, as opposed to staging or development.
(in the plural) Written documents produced in support of the action or defence.
A slip of paper; a short note.
A written or printed table of information, often forming an annex or appendix to a statute or other regulatory instrument, or to a legal contract.
definitions.example: schedule of tribes

definitions.definition: A serial record of items, systematically arranged.
A procedural plan, usually but not necessarily tabular in nature, indicating a sequence of operations and the planned times at which those operations are to occur.
definitions.example: stick to the schedule

definitions.definition: An allocation or ordering of a set of tasks on one or several resources.
To create a time-schedule.
To plan an activity at a specific date or time in the future.
definitions.example: I'll schedule you for three-o'clock then.

definitions.definition: To admit (a person) to hospital as an involuntary patient under the Mental Health Act.
definitions.example: whether or not to schedule a patient

definitions.definition: A ship that has sunk or run aground so that it is no longer seaworthy.
An event where a ship sinks or runs aground.
Destruction; ruin; irretrievable loss
To wreck a boat through a collision or mishap.
(in the plural) The practicing members of a religion or followers of a cause.
definitions.example: The faithful pray five times a day.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that is faithful or reliable.
Loyal; adhering firmly to person or cause.
definitions.example: My dog is a very faithful dog: he doesn't like to be petted by anybody else.

definitions.definition: Having faith.
Reliable; worthy of trust.
definitions.example: My servant is very faithful.

definitions.definition: Consistent with reality.
definitions.example: I would consider that a very faithful reproduction.

definitions.definition: Engaging in sexual relations only with one's spouse or long-term sexual partner.
definitions.example: They had been faithful to each other all of their married life.

definitions.definition: (mathematics) Injective in specific contexts, e.g. of representations in representation or functors in category theory.
The imagination.
An image or representation of anything formed in the mind.
An opinion or notion formed without much reflection.
A whim.
definitions.example: I had a fancy to learn to play the flute.

definitions.definition: Love or amorous attachment.
definitions.example: He took a fancy to her.

definitions.definition: The object of inclination or liking.
Any sport or hobby pursued by a group.
definitions.example: Trainspotting is the fancy of a special lot.

definitions.definition: The enthusiasts of such a pursuit.
definitions.example: He fell out of favor with the boxing fancy after the incident.

definitions.definition: A diamond with a distinctive colour.
That which pleases or entertains the taste or caprice without much use or value.
A sort of love song or light impromptu ballad.
In the game of jacks, a style of play involving additional actions (contrasted with plainsies).
Decorative.
definitions.example: This is a fancy shawl.

definitions.definition: Of a superior grade.
definitions.example: This box contains bottles of the fancy grade of jelly.

definitions.definition: Executed with skill.
definitions.example: He initiated the game winning play with a fancy, deked saucer pass to the winger.

definitions.definition: Unnecessarily complicated.
definitions.example: I'm not keen on him and his fancy ideas.

definitions.definition: Extravagant; above real value.
In a fancy manner; fancily.
An area of land that is at elevation; mountainous land.
Relating to highlands.
A person or creature that is active at night.
A device for telling the time at night, rather like a sundial but read according to the stars.
(of a person, creature, group, or species) Primarily active during the night.
definitions.example: nocturnal birds

definitions.definition: (of an occurrence) Taking place at night, nightly.
definitions.example: a suspicious nocturnal outing

definitions.definition: A chemical element (symbol Nd) with an atomic number of 60: a hard, slightly malleable silvery metal that quickly tarnishes in air and moisture.
A single atom of this element.
The quality of being reliable, dependable or trustworthy.

The ability to measure the same thing consistently (of a measurement indicating the degree to which the measure is consistent); that is, repeated measurements would give the same result (See also validity).
Measurable time of work before failure
The act of stretching or extending; extension.
The ability to reach or touch with the person, a limb, or something held or thrown.
definitions.example: The fruit is beyond my reach.

definitions.definition: The power of stretching out or extending action, influence, or the like; power of attainment or management; extent of force or capacity.
Extent; stretch; expanse; hence, application; influence; result; scope.
An exaggeration; an extension beyond evidence or normal; a stretch.
definitions.example: To call George eloquent is certainly a reach.

definitions.definition: The distance a boxer's arm can extend to land a blow.
Any point of sail in which the wind comes from the side of a vessel, excluding close-hauled.
The distance traversed between tacks.

A stretch of a watercourse which can be sailed in one reach (in the previous sense). An extended portion of water; a stretch; a straightish portion of a stream, river, or arm of the sea extending up into the land, as from one turn to another. By extension, the adjacent land.
A level stretch of a watercourse, as between rapids in a river or locks in a canal. (examples?)
An extended portion or area of land or water.
An article to obtain an advantage.
The pole or rod connecting the rear axle with the forward bolster of a wagon.
An effort to vomit; a retching.
To extend, stretch, or thrust out (for example a limb or object held in the hand).
definitions.example: He reached for a weapon that was on the table.

definitions.definition: To give to someone by stretching out a limb, especially the hand; to give with the hand; to pass to another person; to hand over.
definitions.example: to reach one a book

definitions.definition: To stretch out the hand.
To attain or obtain by stretching forth the hand; to extend some part of the body, or something held, so as to touch, strike, grasp, etc.
definitions.example: The gun was stored in a small box on a high closet shelf, but the boy managed to reach it by climbing on other boxes.

definitions.definition: To strike or touch with a missile.
definitions.example: His bullet reached its intended target.

definitions.definition: Hence, to extend an action, effort, or influence to; to penetrate to; to pierce, or cut.
To extend to; to stretch out as far as; to touch by virtue of extent.
definitions.example: When the forest reaches the river, you will be able to rest.

definitions.definition: To arrive at (a place) by effort of any kind.
definitions.example: After three years, he reached the position of manager.

definitions.definition: To make contact with.
definitions.example: I tried to reach you all day.

definitions.definition: To connect with (someone) on an emotional level, making them receptive of (one); to get through to (someone).
definitions.example: What will it take for me to reach him?

definitions.definition: To arrive at a particular destination.
To continue living until, or up to, a certain age.
definitions.example: You can only access the inheritance money when you reach the age of 25.

definitions.definition: To understand; to comprehend.
To overreach; to deceive.
To strain after something; to make (sometimes futile or pretentious) efforts.
definitions.example: Reach for the stars!

definitions.definition: To extend in dimension, time etc.; to stretch out continuously (past, beyond, above, from etc. something).
To sail on the wind, as from one point of tacking to another, or with the wind nearly abeam.
To experience a vomiting reflex; to gag; to retch.
A violent revolt by peasants.

A method of self-defence established in Japan emphasizing “jū” (soft or gentle) “jutsu” (art or technique). Japanese martial art that utilizes a large variety of techniques in defense against an opponent.
Brazilian jiu-jitsu, a related art, derived from judo.
Precision maneuvering.
A prostitute's client.
A device or place to urinate and defecate: now usually a toilet or lavatory, but also a chamber pot or outhouse.
A Western man traveling in East Asia.
A male mule.
A condom.
An inexperienced new worker, usually an immigrant.
A hospital gown: a gown with a back opening closed with snaps or ties, worn by hospital patients.
An insane or eccentric person; a crackpot.
Eccentric behaviour; lunacy.
Flawed or damaged; unsound, liable to break apart; ramshackle.
Sickly, frail; diseased.
Of unsound mind; insane, demented.
definitions.example: His ideas were both frightening and crazy.

definitions.definition: Out of control.
definitions.example: When she gets on the motorcycle she goes crazy.

definitions.definition: Very excited or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He went crazy when he won.

definitions.definition: In love; experiencing romantic feelings.
definitions.example: Why is she so crazy about him?

definitions.definition: Very unexpected; wildly surprising.
definitions.example: The game had a crazy ending.

definitions.definition: Very, extremely.
definitions.example: That trick was crazy good.

definitions.definition: Any specific chemical element or chemical compound or alloy.
An artificial chemical compound.
An addictive drug.
Of or relating to chemistry.
Of or relating to a material or processes not commonly found in nature or in a particular product.
Of or relating to alchemy.
The process of making an estimate.
The amount, extent, position, size, or value reached in an estimate.
Esteem or favourable regard.
A process of choosing a leader, members of parliament, councillors or other representatives by popular vote.
definitions.example: How did you vote in (UK also: at) the last election?

definitions.definition: The choice of a leader or representative by popular vote.
definitions.example: The election of John Smith was due to his broad appeal.

definitions.definition: An option that is selected.
definitions.example: W-4 election

definitions.definition: Any conscious choice.
In Calvinism, God's predestination of saints including all of the elect.
Those who are elected.
The note on which a musical key is based; the tonic.
The main theme of a speech, a written work, or a conference.
A speech that sets the main theme of a conference or other gathering; a keynote speech or keynote address.
To deliver a speech that sets the main theme of a conference or other gathering.
Alternative form of Kraken

A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.
Dramatic irony: a theatrical effect in which the meaning of a situation, or some incongruity in the plot, is understood by the audience, but not by the characters in the play.
Ignorance feigned for the purpose of confounding or provoking an antagonist; Socratic irony.
The state of two usually unrelated entities, parties, actions, etc. being related through a common connection in an uncommon way.
Contradiction between circumstances and expectations; condition contrary to what might be expected.
(grammar) A word that modifies a noun or describes a noun’s referent.
definitions.example: The words “big” and “heavy” are English adjectives.

definitions.definition: A dependent; an accessory.
To make an adjective of; to form or convert into an adjective.
(chiefly as a participle) To characterize with an adjective; to describe by using an adjective.
Incapable of independent function.
(grammar) Adjectival; pertaining to or functioning as an adjective.
Applying to methods of enforcement and rules of procedure.
(of a dye) Needing the use of a mordant to be made fast to that which is being dyed.
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
Fragmented, in separate pieces.

definitions.definition: Non-functional; not functioning properly.

definitions.definition: Having no money; bankrupt, broke.
(of land) Uneven.

An answer to any action.
A memorandum of points of fact or of law for use in conducting a case.

(English law) The material relevant to a case, delivered by a solicitor to the barrister who tries the case.
A short news story or report.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) undershorts briefs.

definitions.definition: A summary, précis or epitome; an abridgement or abstract.
A letter patent, from proper authority, authorizing a collection or charitable contribution of money in churches, for any public or private purpose.
(slang) A ticket of any type.

definitions.definition: To write a legal argument and submit it to a court.
Of short duration; happening quickly.

definitions.definition: Concise; taking few words.

definitions.definition: Occupying a small distance, area or spatial extent; short.

definitions.definition: Rife; common; prevalent.
Briefly.
Soon; quickly.

definitions.definition: A method or way of organizing or planning.

definitions.definition: Make sad or dispirited.
To cast down.

definitions.definition: An almost white colour, with tints of grey, yellow or brown; originally that of natural fabric.
One with a simple mind; a fool or idiot.
One's natural life.
A hairstyle for people with afro-textured hair in which the hair is not straightened or otherwise treated.
Closed under submodules, direct sums, and injective hulls.
That exists and evolved within the confines of an ecosystem.

definitions.definition: Without artificial additives.

A car carrier or car transporter truck or truck-and-trailer combination
A lowboy trailer
(tempering) A device sending a copious stream of water to the heated surface of a bulky object, such as an anvil or die.
The act of flowing; flux; flow.
A quantity of earth, eighteen feet square and one foot deep.
A polishing block used in marble working; a runner.
A coal cart.
A breakdancing move in which the body is held parallel to the floor while balancing on one or both hands.
A visual style on a web page that causes the styled elements to float above or beside others.
Of an object or substance, to be supported by a liquid of greater density than the object so as that part of the object or substance remains above the surface.
definitions.example: The boat floated on the water.

definitions.definition: To cause something to be suspended in a liquid of greater density.
definitions.example: to float a boat

definitions.definition: To be capable of floating.
definitions.example: Oil floats on vinegar.

definitions.definition: To move in a particular direction with the liquid in which one is floating
definitions.example: I’d love to just float downstream.

definitions.definition: To drift or wander aimlessly.
definitions.example: Images from my childhood floated through my mind.

definitions.definition: To drift gently through the air.
definitions.example: The balloon floated off into the distance.

definitions.definition: To move in a fluid manner.
definitions.example: The dancer floated gracefully around the stage.

definitions.definition: To circulate.
definitions.example: There's a rumour floating around the office that Jan is pregnant.

definitions.definition: (of an idea or scheme) To be viable.
definitions.example: That’s a daft idea... it’ll never float.

definitions.definition: To propose (an idea) for consideration.
definitions.example: I floated the idea of free ice-cream on Fridays, but no one was interested.

definitions.definition: To automatically adjust a parameter as related parameters change.
(of currencies) To have an exchange value determined by the markets as opposed to by rule.
definitions.example: The yen floats against the dollar.

definitions.definition: To allow (the exchange value of a currency) to be determined by the markets.
definitions.example: Increased pressure on Thailand’s currency, the baht, in 1997 led to a crisis that forced the government to float the currency.

definitions.definition: To extend a short-term loan to.
definitions.example: Could you float me $50 until payday?

definitions.definition: To issue or sell shares in a company (or units in a trust) to members of the public, followed by listing on a stock exchange.
definitions.example: 2007, Jonathan Reuvid, Floating Your Company: The Essential Guide to Going Public.

definitions.definition: To spread plaster over (a surface), using the tool called a float.
To use a float (rasp-like tool) upon.
definitions.example: It is time to float this horse's teeth.

definitions.definition: To transport by float (vehicular trailer).
To perform a float.
To cause (an element within a document) to float above or beside others.
A hereditary ruler; a hereditary peer in the House of Lords.
Passed on as an inheritance, by last will or intestate.
Of a title, honor or right: legally granted to somebody's descendant after that person's death.
definitions.example: Duke is a hereditary title which was created in Norman times.

definitions.definition: Of a person: holding a legally hereditary title or rank.
definitions.example: hereditary rulers

definitions.definition: Of a disease or trait: passed from a parent to offspring in the genes
definitions.example: Haemophilia is hereditary in his family.

definitions.definition: Of a ring: such that all submodules of projective modules over the ring are also projective.
A nightjar, especially Caprimulgus europaeus.
A New World nightjar of the genus Chordeiles, especially Chordeiles minor.
A person whose preference or custom is to remain awake and active during the night and the early morning hours.
A nighthawker.
One who ranges; a rover.
A keeper, guardian, or soldier who ranges over a region (generally of wilderness) to protect the area or enforce the law.
That which separates or arranges; a sieve.
A dog that beats the ground in search of game.
In some modern armies, an elite soldier, similar to special forces but often operating in larger units.
A warrior character, often with wilderness and stealth skill, who typically travels the countryside.
A character skilled in the use of ranged weapons.
The act of travelling or sightseeing, particularly away from one's home.
The industry in which such travels and sightseeing are organized.
Collectively, the tourists visiting a place or landmark.
The act of visiting another region or jurisdiction for a particular purpose.
definitions.example: libel tourism; suicide tourism; sex tourism
